findings.description: 12 xfail markers with strict=False silently swallowed xpass in test_data_pipeline.py. Infrastructure improvements would go unnoticed.
wait_for_service treated any status_code < 500 as healthy. Polaris management endpoint returns 503 when not ready, but 401/404 from misconfiguration would also pass.
findings.proposedRule: Health-readiness endpoints use strict_status=True for exact 200 validation

findings.description: Migrating Polaris test from port 8181 to 8182 deleted HTTP validation of catalog API port. Coverage subtracted silently.
findings.proposedRule: When migrating test targets, verify old target is still tested elsewhere. Test BOTH if not.

findings.description: Two kubectl port-forward processes targeting same service created race condition and dual PID management.
findings.proposedRule: Use single kubectl port-forward with multiple port pairs for same-service multi-port forwarding

findings.description: Governance enforcement had 3 positive-path tests but zero negative-path tests. A hardcoded passed=True implementation would pass all tests.
findings.proposedRule: Enforcement/validation logic MUST have negative-path tests verifying rejection content, not just exception type

findings.description: 5-gate verification caught actionable findings in 7 of 8 WUs. gate-tests found the most issues. Total: 8 BLOCKs, 61 WARNs across epic.
findings.proposedRule: Never skip 5-gate verification, even for simple changes. gate-tests (adversarial auditor) is the highest-yield gate.
